qa_pairs.question: Why is it recommended to migrate systems and platforms to newer releases on Alliance Clouds?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to migrate systems and platforms to newer releases on Alliance Clouds to ensure continued reception of patches and security updates. This is because older releases have an End-of-Life (EOL) after which support and updates are no longer provided.

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy regarding older Linux distribution images on Alliance Clouds once they pass their End-of-Life (EOL)?
qa_pairs.answer: Older images for Linux distributions that have passed their End-of-Life (EOL) will be removed from Alliance Clouds.

qa_pairs.question: After an End-of-Life (EOL) Linux distribution image is removed from Alliance Clouds, can new virtual machines (VMs) still be created with it?
qa_pairs.answer: No, after an End-of-Life (EOL) Linux distribution image is removed from Alliance Clouds, it will no longer be available for creating new virtual machines (VMs). However, existing VMs running an EOL Linux distribution can continue to run, though this is not recommended.
